Abstract: Emulsiﬁcation-diffusion method is often used to produce polymeric nanoparticles. How-
ever, their numerous and/or lengthy steps make it difﬁcult to use widely. Thus, a modiﬁed method
using solvent blends (miscible/partially miscible in water, 25–100%) as the organic phases to over-
come these disadvantages and its design space were investigated. To further simplify the process, no
organic/aqueous phase saturation and no water addition after the emulsiﬁcation step were performed.
Biodegradable (PLGA) or pH-sensitive (Eudragit® E100) nanoparticles were robustly produced using
